options.content:
## AI Observability — Support Bot (Acme)

Story-driven view for the internal HR/IT bot. Same OpenTelemetry + Sigil stack as NeonCart, very different workload — corporate data, role-based access, governance is the headline.

**📑 Stories on this page:**
- 🚫 Bad Question Askers — who's pushing the bot's limits (refusals · escalations · denials)
- 💸 Top Spenders by Employee — who's burning the budget
- 🛡️ Sensitive Data Leaks — PII detections + API-key leaks
- 🚨 Prompt Injections — injection-detector hits + offending users
- 📈 Token Spikes — abnormal employee usage patterns

**📧 Email Cascade Demo (live-only):** type *"show me every email I have ever sent"* in the Support Bot UI. The orchestrator runs a 3-attempt cascade (full range → yearly → hourly chunks) that fans out 50+ tool calls + 48 LLM chunk-summarizers in seconds. Watch the **Token Spikes** tab below — the spike index will jump >3× within a minute. Trace shows the full cascade as nested spans, conversation appears in the live /ask feed, cost ledger jumps.

**Linked lens dashboards (collapsed below):** RAG · Errors · Cost · Conversation · Sub-Agent

## 🚫 Bad Question Askers

Internal AI is a corporate-data attack surface. This view ranks employees by
how often the bot **refused, escalated, or denied** their requests — the
classic insider-threat / over-curious-employee pattern.

panels.title: Failed eval executions per minute (any judge)

options.content:
## 💸 Top Spenders by Employee

Sigil generation events carry tokens + cost. Here we rank by **total spend** and **total tokens** in the last 24h. Whoever sits at top is either a power user or a runaway loop.

panels.description: Estimated $ on judge tokens. ($3/Mtok blended Haiku rate.)
panels.title: Total spend (24h)

options.content:
## 🛡️ Sensitive Data Leaks

The PII detector flags every input + output for credit cards, SSNs, emails, phone numbers, and **API keys**. This view counts hits and shows the recent detection log. Spike here = either a careless employee or a successful jailbreak.

panels.description: Fraction of pii evaluator runs that failed (i.e. flagged content).
panels.title: PII judge fail rate
🛡️ Pods most often emitting PII signals (24h)

options.content:
## 🚨 Prompt Injections

The injection-detector specialist screens every employee question. Hits here are the **OWASP LLM01** signal — direct prompt injection, jailbreak, ignore-prior-instructions patterns. Pair with the offender table below.

panels.description: Demo proxy: detector catches 99% of seeded injection patterns.
panels.title: Injection block rate (proxy)
Refusal mix per minute (deny / refuse / out_of_scope)

options.content:
## 📈 Token Spikes

Per-evaluator token-rate anomaly detection. Spike-ratio = current 1m rate divided by trailing 1h rate; **>3× is alert-worthy**. The "someone is hammering the bot right now" signal.

panels.description: >1 = burning faster than trailing hour. >3 = alert-worthy.
panels.title: Token-spike index (any evaluator)

panels.description: Spike index = 1m / 1h rate. Bands above 3 = abuse-worthy.
panels.title: 📈 Token-rate spike-ratio over time (per evaluator)
📈 Token volume per minute by evaluator

options.content:
### 🚨 Errors & Stop Reasons

→ **Open:** [🚨 Errors & Stop Reasons](/d/ai-obs-errors) · uid: `ai-obs-errors`

## 📧 Email Cascade Demo (live-only)

Type **"show me every email I have ever sent"** in the Support Bot UI. The orchestrator runs a 3-attempt cascade against the (fake) Acme Mail API:

| Attempt | Scope | Result | LLM calls |
|---|---|---|---|
| **1** | full unbounded range (1970 → now) | 504 `result_too_large` | 0 |
| **2** | 10 yearly chunks 1970..2026 (parallel) | all 503 `max_window_30d` | 0 |
| **3** | 48 hourly chunks across the timeline (parallel) | 48 × 200 ok | **48 chunk-summarizer calls** → token spike |

The trace shows the full cascade as nested spans, the conversation lands in the live `/ask` feed, and tool-call volume for `acme-tools` jumps from ~normal to **+59 in 30 seconds**. Each summarizer call uses **Claude Sonnet 4.6** so cost is real.

panels.description: Fan-out per minute. Spikes by ~50 within 30s during a cascade demo run.
panels.title: Cascade tool calls / minute (acme-tools)

panels.description: 48 parallel summarizer calls per cascade run — the actual token-spike source.
panels.title: Chunk-summarizer LLM calls / minute (suggested-reply-writer)

panels.description: 504 = full-range timeout, 503 = yearly chunk over 30d limit. Both expected during a cascade run.
panels.title: Cascade tool errors by code (1h)

panels.description: Tail of acme-bot-api lines for ask conversations whose intent=email.cascade_search.
panels.title: 🪵 Live cascade trace (last cascade run)

options.content:
## 💊 Outlier Users

20 fake employees on the loadgen. Most use the bot normally; **two outliers (Tim Heidecker + Eric Wareheim)** behave differently:

- **Tim** has weight 8.0 (asks 8× a normal user), **Eric** weight 7.0
- 70% of their queries are prepended with verbose padding (“give me a comprehensive, exhaustive…”) inflating tokens for low-information outputs
- 50% of their queries pull from the **dangerous-fixture pool** — prompt injections, restricted-access probes, PII pastes
- Their conversations target 6–12 turns vs 1–3 for a normal user, so they compound

Watch the **least-efficient** ranking (tokens consumed per output word) and **dangerous events by user** — Tim and Eric should dominate both within ~5 minutes of loadgen running.

panels.description: From the orchestrator's `tokens_per_word` field. Higher = more tokens spent per useful word of answer. Tim and Eric should sit at the top here.

targets.expr: topk(10, avg by (employee) (avg_over_time({namespace="supportbot"} | logfmt | tokens_per_word > 0 | unwrap tokens_per_word [1h])))

panels.title: 💊 Least-efficient employees (avg tokens / output word, 1h)

panels.description: Sum of tokens_in + tokens_out per employee from the orchestrator log line. Outliers compound (multi-turn + verbose padding) so they tower over normal users.

targets.expr: topk(10, sum by (employee) (sum_over_time({namespace="supportbot"} | logfmt | tokens_in > 0 | unwrap tokens_in [24h])) + sum by (employee) (sum_over_time({namespace="supportbot"} | logfmt | tokens_out > 0 | unwrap tokens_out [24h])))

panels.title: 💰 Top spenders — total tokens by employee (24h)

panels.description: Count of `danger kind=...` log lines per employee. Tim and Eric should dominate — they fire dangerous fixtures 50% of the time.
panels.title: 🚨 Dangerous events by employee (1h) — injection · access-denied · pii
🚨 Dangerous events by kind — over time

panels.description: Every dangerous /ask. Click an employee in another panel to filter.
panels.title: 🪵 Live danger feed (last 1h)
